data.title: Designer Brands CEO sees better times ahead as external challenges ease
data.description:
Shares of Designer Brands (NYSE:DBI) have reversed premarket losses and flipped into positive territory as investor sentiment improves following upbeat remarks from the company’s CEO and CFO during the earnings call with analysts.

Shares were trading defensively in the wake of this morning’s Q3 results, when the parent company of DSW reported disappointing Q3 results and lowered its outlook for FY24 on sluggish sales during the latest quarter.

During the earnings call with investors, CEO Doug Howe acknowledged the negative impact the weather had on boot sales and ultimately, overall sales for the company. But excluding boots, sales at DSW were actually up 8% in Q3 from last year, largely on gains in its athletic category. “Seasonality overly penetrated overall sales,” Howe explained, requiring the company to take addition action to adjust seasonal exposure accordingly for 2025.

The fourth quarter is expected to be a strong quarter for comparable sales, said Howe, as he expects the company’s performance to improve as external challenges ease. And by better managing inventory, Designer Brands (DBI) will not need to engage in as much promotional activity going forward.

Shares bottomed out at $4.80 during regular trade, challenging the lower end of the recent trading range, snapping back towards $6 per share, a gain of 3% over Monday's close.

data.title: Watch out below: GameStop is tipped to fall after earnings based on a historical precedent
data.description:
GameStop (NYSE:GME) is due to issue its Q3 earnings report after the closing bell on Tuesday. Analysts expect the retailer to report revenue of $887.7 million (-17.7% year-over-year) and a loss of $0.03 per share.

S3 Partners is a paying attention to one strong pre-earnings signal in particular. The firm noted that both GME's stock price and short interest have risen recently, which has been a historically bearish combination for the earnings share price reaction.

S3's breakdown: "When both metrics—stock price increase and rising short interest—are observed, the stock has historically tended to decline afterward. GME is a highly volatile stock and can move 10% up or down on earnings announcements. The only time GME saw significant gains was when the short interest as a percent of float was slightly lower."

Short interest on GameStop (GME) stands at 7.1% of the total float.

Shares of GameStop (GME) were down 1.6% in afternoon trading on Tuesday. GME has bolted twice this year to above the $40 level but was swapping hands below $28 in front of the earnings release.

data.title: Goldman Sachs would evaluate getting into bitcoin, ether markets, if regulators permit - report
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) Chairman and CEO David Solomon said on Tuesday the Wall Street firm would "evaluate" participating in bitcoin (BTC-USD) or ethereum (ETH-USD) markets if regulators allowed it.

The Goldman chief spoke in an interview at a Reuters event.

He also said that dealmaking in M&A and equities have the potential to top 10-year averages next year. "I think in 2025 we will certainly be at 10-year averages," he said. "We might even be ahead of 10-year average."

He anticipates that the incoming Trump administration will take a "very, very pro-growth agenda," but that will become more clear in the first 100 days, as trade, immigration, energy, and tax policies take shape.

While investment banking has rebounded over the past year, buyouts by private equity firms have been slow. That's expected to pick up next year, he noted.

Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) stock slipped 0.4% in Tuesday afternoon trading. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell 3.3% to $94.6K.

data.title: Equinor, BP, TotalEnergies to move forward with U.K. carbon capture projects
data.description:
Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) and partners BP (NYSE:BP) and TotalEnergies (TTE) said Tuesday they took a final investment decision to develop two of the U.K.'s first carbon capture and storage projects in Teesside in northern England; financial details were not disclosed.

The three companies are partners in the Northern Endurance Partnership, which will permanently transport and store up to an initial 4M metric tons/year of captured carbon dioxide.

Work on the projects is set to begin in 2025, with the 742 MW Net Zero Teesside Power project designed to deliver power to ~1M homes starting in 2028.

Equinor (EQNR) owns a 45% stake in the Northern Endurance Partnership project, while BP (BP) also holds a 45% stake and TotalEnergies (TTE) owns the remaining 10%.

"It is a major milestone to have agreed Final Investment Decision and financial close on two of the U.K.'s first carbon capture, transport and storage infrastructure projects," Equinor (EQNR) executive VP of Marketing, Midstream and Processing Irene Rummelhoff said.

data.title: UBS reshapes U.S. wealth unit in effort to lift profitability - report
data.description:
UBS Group (NYSE:UBS) made organizational changes to its U.S. wealth management business in an effort to drive higher profitability, according to a media report dated Tuesday.

The U.S. wealth advisory structure will shift to four regional units from two divisions, along with the addition of a team dedicated to international clients and an advice center, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing a memo sent to staff by management.

The move comes as UBS (NYSE:UBS) aims to strengthen its banking presence in the U.S., with CEO Sergio Ermotti prioritizing expansion in the region beyond the initial integration of Credit Suisse.

UBS (UBS) also noted it's introducing a new Ultra-High Net Worth Plus segment for clients with assets exceeding $50M, the report said.

(UBS) shares dipped 2.4% in Tuesday afternoon trading.

data.title: Restaurant revelation: Domino's Pizza Group eyes franchise for Wingstop's UK business
data.description:
Domino's Pizza Group (NYSE:DPZ) is reported to be one of four bidders to land control of the UK operations of Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING). The sale process is anticipated to value Wingstop’s UK franchise at around £400 million, according to Financial Times.

The bidding action for the Wingstop (WING) UK business is expected to wrap up on December 16.

Wingstop (WING) entered the UK market in 2018, opening its first restaurant in Central London. The restaurant chain partnered with Lemon Pepper Holdings as its master franchisee for the UK. Since then, Wingstop has experienced rapid growth, expanding to 39 locations by early 2024. In 2021, Wingstop (WING) made a minority investment in its UK franchisee to support further expansion. Wingstop currently runs 56 sites across the UK after opening 17 sites in 2024.

Domino's Pizza (DPZ) is the original American multinational pizza restaurant chain, headquartered in Ann Arbor, Michigan. It operates primarily in the United States and Canada. Meanwhile, Domino's Pizza Group (OTCPK:DPUKY) is the master franchisee for the Domino's Pizza brand in the United Kingdom and Ireland. It is a separate corporate entity that operates under a franchise agreement with the U.S.-based parent company.

data.title: Centene stock slides 4% after Jefferies cuts to underperform
data.description:
Shares of Centene (NYSE:CNC) declined 4% after Jefferies downgraded the stock to underperform from hold, citing industry headwinds over the next two years.

Jefferies noted that Centene’s (CNC) membership and margins have repeatedly topped expectations over the last few years due in part to enhanced subsidies for health insurance exchange, or HIX, plans.

“Looking forward, our industry analysis points to an unwind of this high and profitable growth as the near-term ((broker crackdown)) and long-term ((expiration of enhanced subsidies)) regulatory environment becomes more hostile,” Jefferies said in a note.

The bank said that while Centene (CNC) was the largest Medicaid player, HIX outperformance was now the largest contributor to EPS, with 69% of premiums coming from Federally Facilitated Marketplaces in non-Medicaid Expansion states.

“Like the Medicaid margin pressures from redetermination-driven risk pool changes, we believe these pressures are relatively unanticipated by the HIX players and could cost CNC (CNC) around $1.75 in EPS,” Jefferies added.

The bank lowered its price target for the stock to $52 from $68.

data.title: Chips mostly lower as investors await key results from Broadcom
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks were mostly lower on Tuesday ahead of the latest quarterly results from industry stalwart Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), set to be released later this week.

Broadcom shares fell 4.5% even as Citi upped its price target and reiterated its Buy rating on the Hock Tan-led company. "We expect the company to report results above consensus, driven by a recovery in the non-AI semiconductor business (35% of F24E sales) with a better-than-feared gross margin outlook due to higher software mix," analyst Christopher Danely wrote to clients. "However, we believe F1Q25 guidance will be tempered as there appears to be a slowdown in orders from leading AI customer Google. We do expect business to ramp from Meta throughout C25 and pick up the slack."

Danely raised his price target to $205 from $175 ahead of the results.

A consensus of analysts expects Broadcom to earn $1.39 per share on $14.06B in revenue for the coming quarter.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares reversed premarket gains and fell nearly 4% even as the U.S. Department of Commerce finalized its $6.15B CHIPs Act grant to the memory maker.

The award will support the construction of two fabs in Clay, New York, and one fab in Boise, Idaho as part of Micron’s total $125B investment across both states over the next two decades.

Taiwan Semiconductor shares fell 3% even as the global foundry reported November revenue rose 34% year-over-year, led by strength in the data center. Taiwan Semiconductor produces chips for many of the world's leading companies, including Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL) and AMD (NASDAQ:AMD).

AMD shares fell 1.8% as investors wondered how the company's AI accelerators are performing. On Monday, an AMD spokesperson told Seeking Alpha that last week's report that contained comments made by an Amazon Web Services (AMZN) executives that it has "not yet" seen heavy demand for the former's artificial intelligence accelerators was "not accurate."

AMD competitors Nvidia and Intel (INTC) fell 1.5% and 2.6%, respectively.

A number of other S&P 500 semiconductor companies fell on Tuesday, including Texas Instruments (TXN), Analog Devices (ADI), Qualcomm (QCOM) and NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), all of which fell 2% or more.

data.title: Economy stays resilient, holiday spending firm this year, Citi's Lake says
data.description:
The economy overall continues to be "pretty resilient" as inflation heads in the right direction, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) said Marianne Lake, CEO of Consumer and Community Banking and former CFO of JPMorgan Chase, at an industry conference on Tuesday.

The firm expects the Federal Reserve to cut its benchmark rate next week, then three more cuts by the end of next year.

Holiday spending appears to be solid to a little firmer than it was a year ago. The consumer appears to be on "relatively solid footing," Lake said.

"The honest answer on (lending standards) tightening is: We always are." Any major changes to lending standards will be driven by macroeconomics rather than problems with performance.

Updated at12:35 PM ET: Only areas with pockets of underperformance are in small business credit card and small business lending.

Consumer deposits have dipped, plateauing in October and stabilizing through Q4. "As we have likely seen trough in deposits, we're likely to have seen a peak in CDs," she noted.

From corporations, confidence is stronger, and fears of recession are fading, she added.

Any policy changes on tariffs and immigration from the incoming Trump administration should be "headwinds, not derailers."

Breaking... check back for updates.

data.title: Macy's Q3 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Macy's (NYSE:M) is set to post third quarter earnings on Wednesday, after the company delayed its earnings release in November following an accounting issue related to delivery expenses.

Wall Street expects the department store operator to post EPS of $0.03, indicating an 85.7% decrease, while revenue is expected to be $4.72 billion.

In its preliminary Q3 result, the company said its net sales decreased 2.4% to $4.74 billion, with comparable net sales down 2.4% on an owned basis and down 1.3% on an owned-plus-licensed-plus-marketplace basis.

Macy’s, in August, also lowered its annual sales guidance to reflect a more cautious mood from consumers, amid a tough economic environment.

The company's stock fell nearly 18% so far this year, compared to around 27% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating and Wall Street analysts are cautious and rated the company as a Hold. However, Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish about the firm and rated it as a Buy.

Seeking Alpha analyst The Alpha Sieve noted that Macy’s current avatar could change under Spring's leadership where greater impetus will go towards the luxury segment, where the store count is expected to grow by 20% over the next couple of years.

Over the last two years, Macy's has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 88% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen four upward revisions and three downward revisions. Revenue estimates have seen no upward revisions versus one downward move.
